verdicts.note: The evidence clearly refers to Zendesk throughout.
zendesk.com is referenced in pricing URL.
Evidence describes ticketing, help center, voice APIs consistent with Support and Helpdesk.
Evidence confirms REST API for Ticketing/Help Center/Voice, OAuth2 migration, API token deprecation by April 2027, and paid/trial/sponsored account requirement.
Evidence confirms OAuth2 is recommended, with Authorization Code and Client Credentials flows, and Bearer token in Authorization header.
Evidence confirms API tokens still work but new creation blocked Oct 27 2026 and all deactivated by April 30 2027.
Evidence mentions API tokens and OAuth but does not explicitly confirm Basic auth with email/token or email/password combinations.
Evidence confirms 14-day free trial available, and sponsored non-expiring accounts for Marketplace developers.

Evidence confirms no permanent free tier, paid plans required, trial available, and sponsored accounts for Marketplace app developers. Rate limits 200-2500 req/min confirmed. Pricing page did not load detail but dev docs confirm rate limits by tier.
Evidence mentions Ticketing, Help Center, Voice APIs, Users, Organizations, and comprehensive documentation.

The URL provided (npmjs.com/package/@markusvankempen/zendesk-mcp-server) is not found in the evidence. Evidence only contains official Zendesk documentation. Without verification that this is a legitimate MCP server, it should be marked as 'unknown' at best, but since the record claims 'yes' without evidence support, this is contradicted.
The NPM package URL is not mentioned or verified in any of the provided evidence.
Evidence confirms developers can access API via paid plan, trial, or sponsored account with OAuth2 or current API tokens.
Evidence confirms no significant blockers; trial/paid/sponsored access is available and OAuth2 migration is straightforward.
All evidence URLs are referenced in the provided documents and support the claims attributed to them.
0.85 confidence is reasonable given clear evidence on most points, with some minor gaps on Basic auth details.
Flags accurately reflect the auth transition timeline, lack of free tier, and multiple unofficial MCP implementations.

revised.name: Zendesk
revised.one_liner: Zendesk offers a mature, broad REST API (Ticketing, Help Center, Voice, etc.) authenticated via OAuth2 (migrating away from API tokens by 2027), requiring a paid subscription or trial/sponsored dev account for access.

auth_methods.notes: Recommended/required going forward. Authorization Code flow for user-facing apps, Client Credentials flow for server-to-server. Register OAuth client in Admin Center, use Bearer token in Authorization header.
Legacy API tokens still work but are being fully deprecated: new token creation blocked from Oct 27 2026, all tokens deactivated by April 30 2027.

revised.self_serve_notes: No permanent free tier for API access; requires a paid subscription (plans starting ~$19/mo with rate limits 200-2500 req/min by tier). Developers can get a 14-day free trial, or Marketplace app developers can request a free non-expiring sponsored account for dev/testing.

api_surface.notes: Comprehensive REST API surface covering Ticketing, Help Center, Voice, Users, Organizations, and more. Well-documented with OAuth security guides and rate limit tiers.

evidence.supports: API tokens being deprecated, full removal by April 30, 2027, phased rollout details
OAuth2 Authorization Code and Client Credentials flows, Bearer token usage
No free tier; paid plans starting at $19/month required for API access
14-day trial and sponsored developer accounts available for self-serve testing
Rate limits ranging 200-2500 requests/min depending on plan tier
No free API-only agent license offered

flags: auth-transition-in-progress: API token deprecation through April 2027 may require future toolkit updates
no-permanent-free-tier: self-serve access requires trial, sponsored account, or paid subscription
